docs.title: DELTA SIMULATIONS DOC HUB
docs.text:
Welcome to the Delta Simulations Documentation Hub, for any and all information about our projects

You Should Know

The documentation provided is a Work in Progress, and may feature incomplete information. Please refer to our Discord for the most up-to-date information.

  • UH-60 Blackhawk

    The UH-60 Blackhawk is a four-bladed, twin-engine, medium-lift utility helicopter manufactured by Sikorsky Aircraft.

  • C-17A Globemaster III

    The C-17A Globemaster III is a large military transport aircraft developed by McDonnell Douglas (now Boeing). It's capable of rapid strategic delivery of troops and cargo to main operating bases or forward operating bases.

  • KC-46A Pegasus

    The KC-46A Pegasus is a military aerial refueling and strategic military transport aircraft developed by Boeing.

  • FAQ

    Frequently asked questions about installation, features, and troubleshooting for Delta Simulations aircraft.

docs.title: Contact Us
docs.text:
Please join our Discord

For questions or support, visit our main website: Delta Simulations

docs.title: Downloads
docs.text:
• Download the C-17A Current Development Version

    Click on the deltasimulations-c17.zip file to download

    Download the C-17A

  • Download the UH-60M Current Development Version

    Click on the deltasimulations-uh60.zip file to download

    Download the UH-60M

Please follow ALL steps in this section if you encounter any issues with installation before seeking support. Open and extract the zip that you downloaded from one of the links above, and drag the deltasimulations-XXX folder inside the zip into your Community folder.

See below for the location of your Community folder.

docs.title: Microsoft Store and/or Game Pass Edition
docs.text:
• Copy the deltasimulations-XXX folder into your community package folder.

It is located in:

C:\Users\[YOURUSERNAME]\AppData\Local\Packages\Microsoft.FlightSimulator_<RANDOMLETTERS>\LocalCache\Packages\Community.

docs.title: Steam Edition
docs.text:
• Copy the deltasimulations-XXX folder into your community package folder.

It is located in:

C:\Users\[YOUR USERNAME]\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft Flight Simulator\Packages\Community.

docs.title: Boxed Edition
docs.text:
• Copy the deltasimulations-XXX folder into your community package folder.

It is located in:

C:\Users\[YOUR USERNAME]\AppData\Local\MSFSPackages\Community.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
If the above methods do not work:

To find the Community folder that MSFS is using, please follow these steps:

  1. Go to your General Settings in MSFS and activate Developer Mode.
  2. Go to the menu and select 'Virtual File System'.
  3. Click on 'Packages Folders' and select 'Open Community Folder'. This opens the Community folder in a Windows Explorer. Please ensure that your add-ons are installed in the folder that is opened.

docs.title: Clean Install
docs.text: If you would like to manually perform a clean install you have to delete the deltasimulations-XXX folder from your community folder.

docs.title: How do I report an issue?
docs.text:
CHECK THIS FIRST

We highly recommend joining Our Discord for the fastest response time. Alternatively, please submit a Github Issue on the relevant repo containing the aircraft version, simulator version, and specific, reproducible steps to achieve this issue. Please be as specific as possible.

docs.title: Before Reporting Issues
docs.text:
Please try removing all other mods/liveries from the community folder and test our add-on again. We also reccomend doing a clean install. This will help rule out conflicts.

Most reported issues are caused by conflicts with other mods and liveries, or from an incorrect installation. If this does not resolve your issue, please continue below.

docs.title: How Can I Support Development?
docs.text:
If you are looking to assist with the development, you can start immediately with a PR or fork on our Github, though we highly recommend first filling out our Team Application Form so we can onboard you onto our server, and you can gain access to our reference libraries.

As a freeware organization, we rely on self-funding to cover the software, equipment, and subscriptions required to develop our projects. After years of development and thousands of dollars personally invested, we’re working to make the group more sustainable for the long term.

Donations are the most effective way to support our work if you are not a developer:

Donate via PayPal

Supporters receive a special role on our Discord server along with sneak peeks and additional updates on our development progress. If you donate, please contact a moderator on Discord with proof of donation to receive your role.

Please note: donations do not provide early access to any of our projects — only additional development updates and behind-the-scenes information.

docs.title: Will These Projects Be Available on Xbox?
docs.text: These projects are PC-only at this time, and will remain so for the foreseable future.

docs.title: When I Click a Button/Test, It Doesn't Work as it Should
docs.text: Our aircraft code runs on the PC-based XML scripting which is much easier to debug and adapt for complex aircraft. As a result, you may run into interaction issues when using the XBOX/LOCK control modes. We design our aircraft for use in the LEGACY control mode

docs.title: Are These Projects Free?
docs.text: Yes! This is the heart of our philosophy - our aircrafts will forever be free, open-source, and available to all. We will never gatekeep products behind paywalls or limited access. What you see is what you get.

docs.title: When is the Next Update?
docs.text: We are a team of volunteers developing in our free time and are thus unable to provide an estimate for a release date. We believe in releasing "beta" test builds of our aircraft, allowing for an effective feedback loop. When we believe we have a stable enough build, we will post it in our Discord, and it will be available on Github under the aircraft's releases. Then, you can follow our download instructions available above.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to the UH-60M Blackhawk documentation for Delta Simulations. This guide will help you understand the aircraft systems, flight controls, and operational procedures.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: The UH-60M Blackhawk is a medium-lift utility helicopter used for transport, medical evacuation, and special operations. This guide is intended for virtual simulation purposes using Microsoft Flight Simulator and Delta Simulations’ add-ons.

docs.title: Aircraft Overview
docs.text: Feature Description Simulated Version UH-60M "Mike" w/ UES Crew 2 Pilots + 2 Crew Passengers Up to 11 Max Speed 183 knots (211 mph) Service Ceiling 19,000 ft Engines 2 × T700-GE-701D Turboshaft

docs.title: Simulated Variants
docs.text:
Variants Description UH-60M A base "Mike" model with UES, advanced flight control system, and upgraded rotor blades. Standard configuration for troop transport and utility missions. HH-60M 2 Pilots + 2 Crew with dedicated medical evacuation configuration, including integrated patient treatment system, equipped with an external rescue hoist. HH-60W "Jolly Green II" - The US Air Force's newest Combat Search and Rescue (CSAR) helicopter featuring increased fuel capacity, advanced defensive systems, and improved armor protection for personnel recovery missions. HH-60U "Ghost Hawk" A byproduct of a test cycle, these rarely seen aircraft are used as a testbed for Skunkworks UH-60M modifications and security of the Groom Lake area. MH-60M Piloted exclusively by the U.S. Army's 160th Special Operations Aviation Regiment (SOAR), also known as the Night Stalkers, this special operations variant is equipped with several advanced sensors and capabilities to allow clandestine operations. MH-60M "DAP" The DAP - Direct Action Penetrator, is the weaponised variant of the 160th's MH-60M. Using external stores, it can be equipped with several weapons platforms. S-70i The civilian/international export variant, this can be customized for global customers.

  • Home

  • Startup

docs.title: Aircraft Systems
docs.text: FOR SIMULATION USE ONLY

docs.title: Normal Procedures
docs.text: FOR SIMULATION USE ONLY

docs.title: Before Start
docs.text:
• Parking Brake - Set
  • EGI 1 and EGI 2 switches - ON
    • RAD ALT - ON
  • ICS panels - As required
  • SERVO OFF switch - NORM
    • SERVO SWITCH - NORMAL
    • BACKUP HYD PUMP - AUTO
  • FMS switch - AUTO
  • MFD power switches - OFF
  • ANTI COLLISION, POSITION, FORMATION, and EXT LTS MODE - As required
  • VIB CONT switch - ON
  • CARGO HOOK EMER REL - OPEN
    • CONR ARM switch - SAFE
  • STBY INST switch - TEST
    • BATT GOOD status appears
  • STBY INST switch - ARM
    • ESIS audio tone will activate
  • STBY INST switch - OFF
    • ESIS audio tone will go away
  • BATT NO. 1 and ABTT NO. 2 - TEST
    • BATT GOOD status appears
  • APU GENERATOR switch - OFF
  • NO. 1 and NO.2 GENERATOR switches - ON
  • EXT PWR switch - OFF, unless ground support unit is needed
  • APU CONT switch - OFF
  • FUEL PUMP switch - OFF
  • AIR SCE HT/STRT - APU
  • APU FIRE T-handle - IN
  • FUEL BOOST PUMP switches - OFF
  • ENG EMER OFF T-handles - Full forward

Beeping tones will be heard during the ICS start up checks

  • BATT NO. 1 and NO. 2 switches - ON

docs.title: Cockpit Equipment Checks
docs.text:
• FUEL PUMP switch - APU BOOST
  • APU CONT switch - ON
  • GENERATORS APU switch - ON
  • MFDs - ON
  • Interior/Exterior lighting - Set as required
  • STBY INST switch - ARM
  • EXT PWR switch - OFF and cable disconnected
  • MASTER WARNING PANEL - #1 ENG OUT, #2 ENG OUT, LOW ROTOR R.P.M segment lights are illuminated
  • LAMPS TEST button - Press and check
  • SAS1, SAS2, TRIM, FPS switches - ON
  • Load mission data
    • On MFD, JMVF - Press
    • LOAD DTC soft key - Press
  • HUD - Set as desired
  • Set up MFD/FD/FMS/EFB for mission
    • TACMAP/ND - As required
    • EFB - Configure aircraft to mission

docs.title: Starting Engines
docs.text:
• ENG FUEL SYS selector(s) - As required
  • ENGINE IGNITION switch - ON
  • FUEL BOOST PUMP switches - ON

docs.title: Engine Runup
docs.text:
• ENG POWER CONT lever(s) - FLY
  • GEN 1 FAIL and GEN 2 FAIL cautions disappear above 97% NR
  • Q1 and Q2 - Matched within 5%
  • APU CONT switch - OFF
  • AIR SCE HT/STRT switch - As required
  • ENG FUEL SYS selectors - As required

docs.title: Before Landing
docs.text:
• TAIL WHEEL switch - As required
  • PARKING BRAKE - As required

docs.title: Landing
docs.text:
• Roll-on landing

    A roll-on landing may be used when the helicopter will not sustain a hover or to avoid hovering in snow or dust

  • Slope landing

    The tailwheel should be locked and the parking brake should be set. For slope landings and all ground operations, avoid using combinations of excessive cyclic and low collective settings.

docs.title: After Landing
docs.text:
• TAIL WHEEL switch - As required
  • Exterior lights - As required
  • Avionics/mission equipment - As required

docs.title: Parking and Shutdown
docs.text:
• TAIL WHEEL switch - As required
  • PARKING BRAKE - Set
  • FUEL BOOST PUMP switches - OFF
  • Landing gear - Chocked
  • Mission Equipment - OFF/SAFE/LOCKED as required
  • DEICE, PITOT, ANTI-ICE, and HEATER switches - OFF
  • AIR SCE HT/STRT switch - APU
  • FUEL PUMP switch - APU BOOST
  • APU CONT switch - ON
  • GENERATORS NO. 1 and NO. 2 switches - OFF
  • DTC - Store as required
  • ENG POWER CONT levers - IDLE
  • ENGINE IGNITION switch - OFF
  • BACKUP HYD PUMP switch - OFF
  • ENG POWER CONT levers - OFF
  • ENG FUEL SYS selectors - OFF
  • Rotor brake - As required at or below 40% NR
  • EGI 1 and EGI 2 switches - OFF
  • MFDs - OFF
  • STBY INST switch - OFF
  • Overhead switches - As required
    • ANTICOLLISION/POSITION LIGHTS
    • Lighting controls
    • WINDSHIELD WIPER
    • VENT BLOWER
  • GENERATORS APU switch - OFF
  • FUEL PUMP switch - OFF
  • APU CONT switch - OFF
  • BATT NO.1 and NO.2 switches - OFF

docs.title: Multi Function Display (MFD)
docs.text:
Explains the various pages displayed on the screens in front of the pilot and co-pilot

There are 6 pages that can be displatyed on the MFDs via the bezel keys: 1. Primary Flight Display (PFD)

  1. Primary Flight Display(PFD)

  2. Engine Instrument Caution/Advisory System(EICAS)

  3. Navigation Display(ND)

  4. TAC Map(TAC)

  5. Joint Variable Message Format(JVMF)

  6. Integrated Vehicle Health Management System(IVHMS) - Coming soon
